day3_bumps_status.copy_location: Day 47 version of this file — templates carried forward
day3_bumps_status.note: If any Day-3 bumps were NOT sent Friday: they are now 2 days overdue. Send Monday 2026-05-18 as second priority (after IMAP sweep, before LinkedIn). Still within the 3-7 day window — barely.

day7_new_angle.type: Standalone NEW email — NOT a thread reply
day7_new_angle.subject_type: New subject line — do not inherit Day-0 thread subject
day7_new_angle.imap_precondition: MANDATORY per lead before each send. If INTERESTED reply found: send interested variant (conditional_handling below). If NOT_INTERESTED: cancel permanently.
rules: New standalone email — not a reply to the Day-0 thread
New subject line — different from Day-0. See subject lines per signal below.
Different pain point — NOT a reframe of Day-0. Completely new angle.
First name in subject if known — same rule as Day-0
Town name in subject — local credibility
No question marks in subject — statement of fact
4-6 lines body. Not a long pitch — this is still light touch.
One pain point only — new signal, single clear angle
Do NOT mention the Day-3 bump or Day-0 email explicitly — the new subject line handles the context reset
Reference the original Day-0 finding only briefly ('the Not Secure warning from last week') as a bridge, then pivot hard to the new angle

ssl_pas_day7.day7_angle: Google local search ranking penalty for HTTP sites
ssl_pas_day7.framework: PAS (new Problem: ranking, new Agitate: losing visibility to competitors, new Solution: fix both at once)
subject_options: [First name] — the 'Not Secure' warning is also a Google ranking issue for [business name]
[First name] — why [business name] may be ranking lower in [town] searches
[First name] — [business name]: SSL affects your Google visibility in [town] too
ssl_pas_day7.recommended_subject: [First name] — the 'Not Secure' warning is also a Google ranking issue for [business name]
ssl_pas_day7.body:
[First name],

Sent a note last week about the 'Not Secure' warning on [specific URL from Day-0].

One more thing worth knowing: Google uses HTTPS as a ranking signal for local searches. HTTP sites don't just put visitors off — they rank lower when someone searches for '[trade] in [town]'. So the warning is costing [business name] both trust and visibility at the same time.

Worth sorting before a competitor moves above you. Happy to talk through what the fix looks like.

[Your name]
personalisation_required: specific URL (from Day-0 send records — the exact URL referenced in Day-0)
rules: Use the same specific URL from Day-0 as the anchor — consistency builds credibility
The new angle is ranking, not trust — this is a genuinely different pain point
'Ranking lower in local searches' is verifiable — builds credibility
'Before a competitor moves above you' is competitive framing — low pressure but motivating
Do NOT mention Google Business Profile unless HANDS has confirmed from per-lead research that GBP is incomplete — that is an additional angle, not a replacement
5 lines body max — this is still cold email, not a report

alternative_angle_if_gbp_known_incomplet.trigger: Use ONLY if HANDS can confirm from their research that the lead's Google Business Profile is incomplete or unverified
alternative_angle_if_gbp_known_incomplet.subject: [First name] — [business name]: 'Not Secure' AND an incomplete Google listing
alternative_angle_if_gbp_known_incomplet.body_addition: Add after the ranking paragraph: 'Your Google Business Profile also appears [incomplete / unverified / missing key details] — which compounds the ranking issue. Worth sorting both at the same time.'

mobile_pas_day7.day0_angle: Mobile layout broken / site not loading on mobile screens
mobile_pas_day7.day7_angle: SSL ('Not Secure' warning for all visitors) — or missing click-to-call
mobile_pas_day7.framework: PAS (new Problem: 'Not Secure' for ALL visitors, not just mobile — new and broader)
mobile_pas_day7.condition: Use SSL angle ONLY if the site is confirmed HTTP (which it should be for most Ryedale leads — check Day-0 send records). If site is already HTTPS: use click-to-call angle instead.
subject_options: [First name] — [business name]: one more issue I noticed on the website
[First name] — [business name]'s site also has a 'Not Secure' warning for all visitors
[First name] — [business name]: the mobile issue comes with another problem
mobile_pas_day7.recommended_subject_ssl_angle: [First name] — [business name]: one more issue I noticed on the website
mobile_pas_day7.recommended_subject_click_to_call_angle: [First name] — [business name]: one quick win worth looking at
mobile_pas_day7.body_ssl_angle:
[First name],

Followed up last week about [business name]'s site not loading well on mobile.

Looked at it again — the site is also running on HTTP, which means Chrome shows a 'Not Secure' warning to every visitor, not just mobile users. So the mobile layout issue and the security warning are both working against [business name] at the same time.

Both are a quick fix in the same session. Happy to put a rough number on it if useful.

[Your name]
mobile_pas_day7.body_click_to_call_angle:
[First name],

Followed up last week about [business name]'s site not loading well on mobile.

Noticed one more thing: there's no click-to-call button on the mobile version. Anyone on their phone who wants to get in touch has to copy and paste the number manually — most won't bother.

Easy to add as part of the mobile fix. Happy to talk through what's involved.

[Your name]
rules: SSL angle: only use if Day-0 signal was mobile AND site is HTTP — check send records
Click-to-call angle: use if site is already HTTPS or if SSL angle feels redundant given Day-0
Do NOT repeat the mobile layout description from Day-0 — pivot fully to the new angle
Both angles are 4-5 lines — short and specific

no_website_bab_day7.day0_angle: Business not appearing in Google search for '[trade] [town]'
no_website_bab_day7.day7_angle: Named competitor currently ranking for that search — or specific missed enquiry estimate
no_website_bab_day7.framework: BAB (new Before: competitor is capturing all the calls, After: [business name] captures its share, Bridge: a basic site + GBP is all it takes)
subject_options: Following up — who's getting the [trade] calls in [town]
Following up — [business name]: a competitor is ranking where you could be
[Business name] — [town] [trade] searches: still worth a look
no_website_bab_day7.recommended_subject: Following up — who's getting the [trade] calls in [town]
no_website_bab_day7.subject_note: No first name in subject if owner not known — use business-type framing instead. Never guess a name.
no_website_bab_day7.body_with_named_competitor:
Hi,

Sent a note last week about [business name] not coming up when people search for '[trade] in [town]'.

Checked again this morning — [Competitor Name] in [town/nearby area] is ranking in the top three for that search. Those are enquiries that would naturally go to [business name] if the online presence was there.

Getting a basic site and Google Business Profile live is usually a fortnight's work. Happy to put a rough cost together if you'd like to see what it would take.

[Your name]
no_website_bab_day7.body_without_named_competitor:
Hi,

Sent a note last week about [business name] not coming up when people search for '[trade] in [town]'.

Checked again — businesses from [nearby town / the surrounding area] are showing up ahead in that search. They're getting enquiries that could be going to [business name] instead.

Getting a basic site and Google Business Profile live is usually a fortnight's work. Happy to put a cost together.

[Your name]
personalisation_required: competitor name (if HANDS can identify one from a quick Google search) OR 'nearby area' framing
rules: Specific competitor name is strongly preferred — verifiable and impactful. Takes 30 seconds to Google.
If no clear named competitor: use 'businesses from [nearby town]' — still concrete, still real
Do NOT use 'Hi' and then a first name — use 'Hi' as opener only (no name) if owner unknown
Breakup framing must still feel like an open door — not pressured
'Fortnight's work' framing is UK English and sets realistic expectations

aida_keyword_gap_day7.day0_angle: Business has strong reviews but site not ranking for '[keyword] [town]'
aida_keyword_gap_day7.day7_angle: Review reputation not converting into search visibility — Google amplification opportunity
aida_keyword_gap_day7.framework: AIDA (Attention: great reviews but invisible in search; Interest: competitors with fewer reviews rank above you; Desire: closing the gap would redirect that traffic; Action: 15-minute call)
aida_keyword_gap_day7.allocation: ~5% of Batch 5 sends — test batch only (4.5★+ 100+ reviews qualification)
subject_options: [First name] — [business name] has great reviews but low search visibility in [town]
[First name] — [N] reviews and still not ranking for '[keyword] in [town]'
[First name] — [business name]: the reviews are there, the ranking isn't
aida_keyword_gap_day7.recommended_subject: [First name] — [business name] has [N] great reviews but low search visibility in [town]
aida_keyword_gap_day7.body:
[First name],

Followed up last week about [business name] and the '[keyword] in [town]' ranking.

[Business name] has [N] reviews — more than most competitors in the area. The gap is that Google isn't surfacing the site at the top of local search results, so the reputation isn't converting into as many new enquiries as it could.

A few on-page changes would close that gap. Happy to show you what's holding back the ranking — 15 minutes would cover it.

[Your name]
rules: Use exact review count from Day-0 — credibility anchor, shows real research
Use exact keyword from Day-0 — consistency
'More reviews than most competitors' must be verifiable — check before sending
Shorter is better for high-review leads — they receive more approaches
'15 minutes would cover it' — low-friction call to action for established businesses

if_imap_finds_interested_reply_before_da.action: Cancel Day-7 send. Use interested variant below instead. Reply in thread to their message. Classify. Prepare proposal within 24h.
if_imap_finds_interested_reply_before_da.ssl_interested_variant:
[First name],

Thanks for coming back to me.

Happy to put together a few options and walk you through what makes sense for [business name]. Are you free for a quick call this week?

[Your name]
if_imap_finds_interested_reply_before_da.no_website_interested_variant:
Hi,

Thanks for getting back to me.

I can pull together a few options for getting [business name] visible online. Are you free for a quick call this week?

[Your name]
rules: Reply to their email thread — not a new email
Acknowledge promptly — do not re-pitch the original angle
Offer a call — one ask only
Match their tone and formality level
Log in CHANGELOG same session

day14_breakup.generated_at: 2026-05-17 (Day 51 — EARLY, ahead of Tuesday 2026-05-19 evening schedule)
day14_breakup.type: Breakup email — final touchpoint. Maximum 4 total touchpoints.
day14_breakup.imap_precondition: MANDATORY per lead before each send — same rule as every send.
rules: 3 lines body maximum — no exceptions
No question marks anywhere — subject or body
No pressure — this is a door left open, not a final pitch
No new signal or pain point — do not introduce new issues on Day-14
UK English throughout
Do NOT mention how many times you've followed up — implied, not stated
End with warmth — 'Best of luck' or 'happy to help' — not 'let me know' or 'looking forward to hearing from you'
First name in subject if known (SSL, mobile, AIDA leads) — consistent with full sequence
DO NOT SEND to any lead who replied INTERESTED before this date — use proposal flow instead
This is the 4th and FINAL touchpoint — hard limit. No further contact after Day-14.

ssl_pas_day14.touch_count: 4th and final (Day-0, Day-3 bump, Day-7 new angle, Day-14 breakup)
subject_options: [First name] — closing the loop on [business name]
[First name] — last note from me on [business name]

[Your name]
rules: Reference 'Not Secure' — maintains thread continuity without repeating the full pitch
No specific URL in Day-14 — brief reference only
Do not mention Google ranking angle from Day-7 — this is a clean exit, not a recap
'You know where I am' — warm door open, no friction, no deadline
3 lines — do not add a 4th

subject_options: [First name] — [business name]: last note from me
[First name] — closing the loop on [business name]
[First name] — leaving it with you on [business name]
mobile_pas_day14.recommended_subject: [First name] — [business name]: last note from me

If it's ever worth sorting, happy to help.

'Won't keep nudging' — self-aware, respectful, not apologetic
'Happy to help' — open door, no pressure
Do NOT re-specify the mobile issue — implicit from the sequence context
3 lines only

subject_options: Last note — [business name]
